name: connection_timeout
description: Pipeline fails because it cannot establish or maintain a connection to a required external system (database, API, object storage). This is often transient (network blip, brief service overload) but can indicate persistent infrastructure issues.
immediate_action:
1. Verify that the target service is reachable: ping host, check cloud console for service health.
2. Review connection pool settings — exhausted pools can cause apparent timeouts under load.
3. Check for recent network changes or security group/firewall rule modifications.
4. Retry the pipeline once manually. If it succeeds, the issue was transient.
5. If it fails again: escalate to infrastructure team with the full stack trace and timestamp.
6. If the target is a managed cloud service, open a support ticket with the provider.
prevention: Implement exponential back-off with jitter for all external connections. Set per-request and per-connection timeouts explicitly — never rely on OS defaults. Add a connection health-check step at pipeline startup so failures are caught early, before downstream processing begins. Monitor connection pool utilization with alerts at 70% capacity.
